sanskrit_devanagari: युञ्जन्नेवं सदात्मानं योगी विगतकल्मष: ।सुखेन ब्रह्मसंस्पर्शमत्यन्तं सुखमश्न‍ुते ॥ २८ ॥
sanskrit_iast: yuñjann evaṁ sadātmānaṁyogī vigata-kalmaṣaḥsukhena brahma-saṁsparśamatyantaṁ sukham aśnute

translations.verbatim_quote: Thus the self-controlled yogī, constantly engaged in yoga practice, becomes free from all material contamination and achieves the highest stage of perfect happiness in transcendental loving service to the Lord.

Thus the self-controlled yogi, constantly engaged in yoga practice, becomes free from all material contamination and achieves the highest stage of perfect happiness in transcendental loving service to the Lord.

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt_status: captured (fair-use excerpt; full purport length: 286 chars)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: Self-realization means knowing one’s constitutional position in relationship to the Supreme. The individual soul is part and parcel of the Supreme, and his position is to render transcendental service to the Lord. This transcendental contact with the Supreme is called brahma-saṁsparśa.

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 6.28 Sada yunjan, by constantly concentrating; atmanam, his mind; evam, thus, in the process stated; vigata-kalmasah, the taintles, sinless yogi, free from the obstacles to Yoga; sukhena, easily; asnute, attains; atayantam, absolute-that which exists by transcending limits-, supreme, unsur

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 6.28 Thus, in the above — said manner, devoting himself to the Yoga of the self and by that means expelling all old impurities, the Yogin attains ‘perfect’, i.e., boundless felicity at all times easily, without stress and strain. The felicity is born of the contact with the Brahman (Atman)

positions.rendering: constantly engaged in the practice of yoga and freed from all material contamination, achieves the highest perfectional stage of happiness in transcendental loving service of the Lord
the contact of Brahman, which is supreme happiness — easily attained by constant yoga-practice
contact with the Self in the form of Brahman, which is unsurpassable happiness

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: Brahma-saṁsparśam is read by Advaita as direct contact of consciousness with non-dual Brahman; by Vishishtadvaita as the Self's contact with Brahman as its inner controller; by Gaudiya Vaishnava as the brahma-bhūta stage that precedes loving service. The metaphysical reach exceeds operational engineering, requiring STRETCHED.

literal_meaning: Thus engaging the self always, the yogi free from stain easily attains the touch of Brahman — limitless happiness.
traditional_meaning_consensus: 6.28 completes the result-state from 6.27. Yuñjan evam sadā ātmānam — yoking the self thus, always — the yogi vigata-kalmaṣaḥ (freed of stain) attains brahma-saṁsparśam (the touch / contact of Brahman) sukhena (easily, naturally). The happiness is atyantam — limitless, infinite. Shankara: by constant engagement with yoga, the yogi free of all stain easily achieves the contact of Brahman, which is supreme happiness. Ramanuja: by constantly fixing the mind on the Self, the yogi free of impurity easily attains contact with the Self in the form of Brahman, which is unsurpassable happiness. The metaphysical reach exceeds operational engineering analog. STRETCHED.
